## 3dslibris 2.3.1

This patch release is a stability-focused follow-up to `v2.3.0`, aimed at the real-hardware regressions reported after the menu and navigation changes in that release. The main goal of `v2.3.1` is to make book opening, switching, covers, and fixed-layout rendering behave reliably again on both `.3dsx` and `.cia`, especially on `new3DS`.

### Highlights

- **Book opening and switching are much harder to break**: stale open sessions are gated more carefully, browser-side background work is paused at the right time, and the transition from one book to another no longer relies on timing-sensitive state.
- **Browser and cover behavior are more stable on hardware**: the library no longer corrupts selected-title rendering while warming metadata, and entering a book no longer leaves behind browser artifacts in the reading view.
- **Problematic `PDF`, `CBZ`, and `MOBI` files now prefer safer open/render paths**: this patch deliberately trades some aggressiveness and speed for stability where the `2.3.0` line was proving unreliable in the field.

### Included fixes and behavior changes

- **Opening a book is cancellable and session-safe**: `opening book ...` can now be cancelled with `B`, `Start`, or `Select`, and switching from one book to another no longer depends on stale callbacks or unfinished work from the previous session.
- **Library warmup is better behaved**: list-view metadata/title warmup no longer stalls after only a few entries, and gallery-view marquee/title rendering is less prone to corruption while the browser is still active.
- **EPUB parsing is more robust against real-world files**: the reader handles more malformed or incomplete inputs cleanly, including better named-entity coverage, improved TOC fallback behavior, and safer page-cache handling during interrupted opens and closes.
- **Fixed-layout reading is more defensive**: `PDF` and `CBZ` opening/rendering paths now avoid some of the more fragile preview/render behavior that could leave the app apparently stuck while the first page was being prepared.
- **MOBI opening is stabilized with a conservative fallback path**: on unstable files and runtimes, the reader now prefers a safe text-first open path over richer markup reconstruction. This keeps problematic `.mobi` books opening reliably in the current `2.3.1` line, including the debug `.cia` path on `new3DS`.
- **MOBI tradeoffs are explicit in this patch**: the safe fallback can disable inline MOBI images and can leave the structured MOBI TOC/index unavailable when the HTML-to-text position map cannot be trusted. This is an intentional stability tradeoff for `v2.3.1`, not a silent regression.
- **Reader entry/exit is cleaner overall**: when a book finally opens, the reading screens are less likely to inherit leftover browser drawing state from the previous mode.

### Included assets

- `3dslibris.cia`
- `3dslibris-debug.cia`
- `3dslibris.3dsx`
- `3dslibris-debug.3dsx`
- `3dslibris-sdmc.zip`
- `3dslibris-source.tar.gz`
